verdict.answer_first: A leads with the bottom line: "isn't using HTTP verbs and JSON"; B opens with filler: "Good question".
verdict.decisiveness: A lands on one core standard: "the thing that actually makes an API RESTful... hypermedia"; B dilutes with a maturity table plus a follow-up CTA.
verdict.fabrication: A makes the more specific unverified claim: "GitHub, Stripe, Twitter" are "typically" Level 2; B's broad "Most REST APIs" is also unverified but less specific.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to save as a prescription: "popular checklist" trap + "constraints" principle + HATEOAS example + practical Level 2 distinction.
